name: JIT Access
description: Keep a service dark until a paired browser extension answers a time-based challenge; a valid knock creates a temporary, self-expiring allow entry. Simple mode needs no external dependencies.

USE_JIT_ACCESS.help: Gate this service behind JIT Network Access (dark until a valid knock).
USE_JIT_ACCESS.label: Enable JIT Network Access

JIT_ACCESS_TOKENS.help: Space-separated token kids allowed to unlock THIS service, or '*' for any registered token. Empty = no device may unlock it.
JIT_ACCESS_TOKENS.label: Allowed token kids

JIT_ACCESS_GRANT_TIME.help: Grant lifetime in seconds (e.g. 3600=1h, 7200=2h, 86400=24h). The allow entry expires automatically.
JIT_ACCESS_GRANT_TIME.label: Grant TTL (seconds)

JIT_ACCESS_FAILURE_MODE.help: How an ungranted request is answered. 'interstitial' shows a clear page + detection marker (best UX). 'stealth' returns a generic 404 (gate invisible; Hardened).
JIT_ACCESS_FAILURE_MODE.label: Failure mode

JIT_ACCESS_BINDING.help: Grant binding. 'ip' (Simple) admits the client IP. 'ip+cookie' (Hardened) also requires a device-bound cookie; recommended for shared-egress or no-downstream-auth services.
JIT_ACCESS_BINDING.label: Grant binding

JIT_ACCESS_SKIP_CHECKS.help: If yes, a valid grant short-circuits the rest of the security pipeline (whitelist semantics). Default no keeps WAF/rate-limit/etc. running behind the grant. Enabling this is a sharp edge — pair with ip+cookie.
JIT_ACCESS_SKIP_CHECKS.label: Grant skips remaining checks

JIT_ACCESS_URI_PREFIX.help: Base path for the challenge/respond/enroll endpoints on the protected origin.
JIT_ACCESS_URI_PREFIX.label: Protocol URI prefix

JIT_ACCESS_NONCE_TTL.help: Seconds a challenge nonce remains valid. Dominates knock freshness (there is no client-supplied time field).
JIT_ACCESS_NONCE_TTL.label: Nonce TTL (seconds)

JIT_ACCESS_TRUST_REALIP.help: Hardened: key grants on BunkerWeb's resolved real IP (from USE_REAL_IP / X-Forwarded-For) instead of the TCP peer. Enable ONLY when genuinely behind a trusted proxy with a correctly narrowed REAL_IP_FROM. Default no = key on the un-forgeable TCP peer (immune to X-Forwarded-For / X-Real-IP spoofing).
JIT_ACCESS_TRUST_REALIP.label: Trust resolved real IP (Hardened)

JIT_ACCESS_IPV6_PREFIX.help: IPv6 grant granularity. 128 = exact address (Simple, recommended). 64 = whole /64 segment (Hardened; admits a LAN segment — pair with ip+cookie).
JIT_ACCESS_IPV6_PREFIX.label: IPv6 grant prefix

JIT_ACCESS_RATELIMIT.help: Rate limit for the knock endpoints per source IP. Tune above office/CGNAT scale if many users share an egress IP. Knock failures never feed badbehavior.
JIT_ACCESS_RATELIMIT.label: Knock endpoint rate limit

JIT_ACCESS_ENROLL_TTL.help: Lifetime in seconds of a device enrollment link/code (default 86400 = 24 h, so a user can still enroll the next day). Clamped to 300–604800 (5 min – 7 days). Links survive config reloads; a full nginx restart voids outstanding links.
JIT_ACCESS_ENROLL_TTL.label: Enrollment link lifetime (seconds)

JIT_ACCESS_TOKEN.help: Token registry entry: 'kid:base64url_secret:label[:expiry_unix]'. Add more as JIT_ACCESS_TOKEN_1, _2, ... Simple mode stores these like other server secrets; Hardened encrypts at rest (KEK) or uses asymmetric keys.
JIT_ACCESS_TOKEN.label: Registered token
